summary: Phase 1 Prompt 1.2 MFA Gap Closure — full end-to-end validation (backend + frontend). All 3 security gaps are closed and wired through: (1) disable MFA requires current_password, (2) regenerate backup codes requires password + current TOTP, (3) enforced-role users without MFA are hard-blocked at login via a short-lived mfa_pending token. 21/21 new backend tests pass. Forced-MFA gate UI, role name rendering, direct-URL bypass protection, logout buttons, and ProfileSecurity disable/regenerate modals all pass. mfa_enrolled_at column rename verified via information_schema.
action_items: Pre-existing tests /app/backend/tests/test_auth_rbac.py and /app/backend/tests/test_entities_api.py now fail because their fixtures log in as test-admin/test-director/test-finance and expect a normal access_token. Under the new hard-block these users receive an mfa_pending token (correct per spec) and calls to /api/entities return 401. These fixtures should be updated to either (a) enrol MFA first via pyotp before hitting protected endpoints, or (b) use non-enforced users (test-readonly / test-pm). This is a test-code regression only — the product behaviour is correct.

Optional: consider returning 403 (instead of 401) for mfa_pending tokens hitting non-whitelisted endpoints, since the caller IS authenticated — would disambiguate from expired/missing tokens on the client. (Low priority; spec allows 401/403.)
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py login: mfa_pending lifetime is 15 min — reasonable; cookie is also set for full access only, not for pending (correct, avoids accidental session persistence pre-enrolment).
auth.py mfa_enroll_confirm: on mfa_pending callers it correctly issues a full access token AND sets the httpOnly cookie — token swap complete.
mfa_disable clears mfa_enrolled_at (verified via DB SELECT). mfa_enroll_confirm stamps it (verified).
get_enrollment_principal correctly whitelists only access/mfa_pending token types; other types (mfa_challenge) → 401.
Seniority ordering uses roles.priority ASC; super_admin (priority 1) correctly surfaces as 'Super Administrator' for the gate text.
ForcedMfaEnroll.jsx: logout on intro and mid-setup both navigate cleanly to /login; QR data-URI renders and secret reveal element exists.
Direct URL navigation to /entities while on pending-gate is correctly kept on the gate (ProtectedRoute wrapping).
test_credentials: See /app/memory/test_credentials.md — all test users use TestUser-Dev-2026!
seed_data_creation: Ran python scripts/seed_test_users.py at start and end of the session (idempotent). Each test class resets target user's MFA state directly via SQL (_reset_user_mfa helper) so tests are hermetic.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_mfa_gap_closure.py is the authoritative regression for the Prompt 1.2 addendum. It covers login hard-block, pending-token gating, enrolment promotion, disable password-gate, regenerate password+TOTP-gate, and DB column rename. Run with: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_mfa_gap_closure.py -v. The old test_auth_rbac.py / test_entities_api.py fixtures are stale re: enforced-role tokens and should be updated before re-running the full suite. ForcedMfaEnroll UI test uses data-testid selectors: forced-mfa-gate, forced-mfa-role, forced-mfa-setup-btn, forced-mfa-qr, forced-mfa-logout-btn, forced-mfa-logout-mid.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no product bugs found. Only stale test-file regressions in test_auth_rbac.py / test_entities_api.py flagged as action item.
